name: Climb to Peña Amaya and visit its Cave — circular through Valdelucio lands
name_original: Subida a Peña Amaya y visita a su Cueva — circular por tierras de Valdelucio
region_intro: Hiking around Sotresgudo offers access to diverse natural landscapes in the province of Burgos, characterized by unique geological formations. The area serves as a base for exploring the nearby Las Tuerces Natural Area, known for its karst landscape with stone mushrooms and natural bridges, and the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, featuring deep gorges carved by rivers and the southernmost foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. These natural parks provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Sotresgudo The most popular hiking route is Climb to Peña Amaya and visit its Cave — circular through Valdelucio lands, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete, with notable geological features and views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fuente la Gallina and Mojón de la Abadesa Loop, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.9 km) path offering varied terrain and scenic views. Local hikers also love the Canal de Castilla Circular from Herrera de Pisuerga, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail following the historic waterway, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Hiking around Sotresgudo is defined by karst landscapes, deep river gorges, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Sotresgudo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Sotresgudo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sotresgudo?
A: There are over 18 hiking routes available around Sotresgudo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These trails explore the unique geological formations and natural parks of the region.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Sotresgudo?
A: Hiking near Sotresgudo offers diverse landscapes, from the whimsical karst formations of the Las Tuerces Natural Area, featuring 'stone mushrooms' and natural bridges, to the deep gorges carved by rivers in the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. You'll encounter the southernmost foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains, providing varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067701541/canal-de-castilla-circular-from-herrera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Circular from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8-mile (7.7 km) trail following a historic waterway, perfect for a relaxed outing. In total, there are 3 easy routes in the area.

Q: Are there more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes around Sotresgudo. These trails often involve more significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sotresgudo?
A: The trails around Sotresgudo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique geological features, the tranquility of the natural parks, and the diverse scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Sotresgudo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046650221/climb-to-pena-amaya-and-visit-its-cave-circular-through-valdelucio-lands" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Peña Amaya and visit its Cave — circular through Valdelucio lands&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) loop offering notable geological features and views.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while hiking near Sotresgudo?
A: The region is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the unique karst landscape of Las Tuerces, with its 'stone mushrooms' and natural bridges. The nearby La Horadada Canyon, carved by the Pisuerga River, is another geological highlight. Don't miss &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4045496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular natural monuments.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5831684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt; offer a glimpse into historical engineering. Additionally, you might find sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gama Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4381459" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CastroAmaya&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in the Sotresgudo area?
A: Many trails in the natural areas around Sotresgudo are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What unique geological features should I look out for?
A: The region is famous for its geology. In Las Tuerces, look for the spectacular 'stone mushrooms' and natural arches formed by millennia of erosion. In Montes Obarenes, you'll witness dramatic structural relief and deep gorges carved by rivers like the Ebro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046650221/climb-to-pena-amaya-and-visit-its-cave-circular-through-valdelucio-lands" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Peña Amaya&lt;/a&gt; route also features notable geological formations.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Both the Las Tuerces Natural Area and Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park are rich in biodiversity. You might spot golden eagles, peregrine falcons, foxes, wild boars, and roe deer. Montes Obarenes is particularly known for its significant population of griffon vultures, along with other birds of prey, wild cats, and otters.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sotresgudo?
A: The best time to hike in Sotresgudo is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn offers beautiful colors. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still pleasant. Winters can be cold, but offer a different, serene beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that follow waterways or canals?
A: Yes, the historic Canal de Castilla offers excellent walking opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067701541/canal-de-castilla-circular-from-herrera-de-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Circular from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309619927/barrio-de-san-vicente-by-the-canal-de-castilla-from-the-centro-de-interpretacion-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrio de San Vicente by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; also follows this historic waterway, providing flat and scenic paths.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views in the Sotresgudo area?
A: For panoramic views, head to the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, where the southernmost foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains offer extensive vistas. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046650221/climb-to-pena-amaya-and-visit-its-cave-circular-through-valdelucio-lands" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Peña Amaya&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent viewpoints over the surrounding Valdelucio lands and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amaya&lt;/a&gt; summit.
tips: Route through Peña Amaya. There are parrots, canyons, caves, ruinous landscapes, waterfalls... a true spectacle. La Cascada is on a track that goes to the right just before entering the town.
The Amaya rock (1,370 m) is the most prominent peak in the northwestern corner of the province of Burgos.
Peña Amaya: a colossus on the horizon... In a watchful and spectacular watchtower over a thousand meters high.
In Sotresgudo (Burgos), as in many other towns in the north of the province, there are vestiges of the Spanish Civil War, including trenches and fortifications.
